mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly increasing the present value of equity cash flows and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.2 percent within a single quarter, it signals a labor market inflection that historically precedes equity drawdowns as earnings growth expectations collapse over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 60 percent of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the signal shows no predictive power in post-2000 data.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in housing starts and industrial production indicates a broad-based demand-supply mismatch that compresses corporate margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when tested against a random baseline over a 5-year out-of-sample period, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression shows a p-value greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-rate environment where the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0 percent, accelerating core inflation measured by CPIAUCSL forces the market to price in prolonged liquidity withdrawal, depressing equity valuations over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if CPIAUCSL increases while SPX 5-day returns remain positive in more than 50 percent of observations during the restrictive regime, or if the cross-sectional correlation drops below -0.15.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts reflect increased business confidence and consumer spending capacity, which historically translates into higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day window.
failure_condition: The signal produces a negative rank IC over any 3-year rolling window, or housing starts increase while SPX 20-day returns decline in more than 60 percent of monthly observations.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS growth decelerates below 2.5 percent year-over-year, it signals a credit crunch that restricts consumer financing and drags down equity valuations over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS annual growth rate falls below 2.5 percent
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55 percent of instances following the trigger, or the signal predictive accuracy does not exceed 50 percent over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where payroll employment growth exceeds 200,000 per month while real GDP growth stagnates below 1.5 percent indicates an unsustainable labor boom that will trigger wage-push inflation and subsequent equity sell-offs over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when tested against a random baseline over a 5-year out-of-sample period, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression shows a p-value greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of industrial production growth above 105, a sudden spike in the unemployment rate above 4.0 percent signals a structural labor shortage that bottlenecks supply chains, compressing margins and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if INDPRO remains above 105 while SPX 5-day returns remain positive in more than 50 percent of observations during the regime, or if the cross-sectional correlation drops below -0.15.
